verdict_reasoning: SRC canonically anchors to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation. However, it undergoes noncanonical topological inversion onto the cell surface in cancer cells through autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE), reaching the outer leaflet where it becomes accessible to bispecific TCEs, ADCs, and radioligands. This surface presentation is documented in cancer cell lines, xenografts, and primary tumors, and depends on dysregulated autophagic flux (a cancer hallmark). The protein body reaches the outer face via its own topological-inversion mechanism during the cancer state, not via transient recruitment to other surface proteins.
